name: Lemon Anise Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift the flour, baking powder, and salt into a small bowl.
2. In a large bowl, whisk together the sugar and eggs until light in color.
3. Stir in the vanilla, lemon zest, and anise seed.
4. Sift the dry ingredients over the egg mixture and fold in until just combined.
5. Adjust the oven rack to the middle position and preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
6. Halve the dough and shape each portion into a rough log on an oiled cookie sheet covered with parchment paper.
7. Place the logs about 3 inches apart on the cookie sheet.
8. Pat each dough shape to smooth it out.
9. Bake for 35 minutes, or until the loaves are golden and beginning to crack on top.
10. Cool the loaves for 10 minutes.
11. Lower the oven temperature to 325°F (160°C).
12. Cut each loaf diagonally into 3/8-inch slices with a serrated knife.
13. Arrange the slices about half an inch apart on a baking sheet, cut side up.
14. Return the slices to the oven and bake for another 15 minutes, turning them over halfway through the baking time, until crisp and golden brown on both sides.
15. Transfer the biscuits to a wire rack and allow them to cool completely. They can be stored in an airtight container for up to one month.
16. Variation: Substitute unhulled sesame seeds for anise seeds. Brush the tops with egg wash and sprinkle with additional sesame seeds.
